verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is canonically a mitochondrial F1 subunit residing in the mitochondrial matrix-facing catalytic head. However, multiple independent groups have documented ectopic display of ATP synthase beta subunit on the plasma membrane outer leaflet, particularly on tumor cells, hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and adipocytes — where it acts as a receptor for apoA-I, angiostatin, and other ligands. This surface pool is not a GPI-anchored or TM-domain-based form, but functional cell-surface ATP synthase complexes have been detected by flow cytometry and antibody labeling. This documented, cell-type-restricted outer-face localization qualifies as contextual rather than constitutive.
